title: pithecellobium rufescens
intro: Pithecellobium rufescens, also known as the coral tree, is a flowering plant species native to Central and South America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat a range of ailments. The health benefits of Pithecellobium rufescens include the ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
